message:
fix(security): harden input validation across AWS, Cloudflare, DockerHub, GCP, Netlify, PayPal, and Vercel connectors

Unified error handling and input sanitization improvements:

- AWS: Switch from AwsError::Api{code,message} to AwsError::InvalidInput
  for validation errors. Add dedicated sanitize_object_key() that allows
  forward slashes (valid in S3 hierarchical keys like photos/2024/img.jpg)
  while still blocking backslash and ".." path traversal. S3 get/put/delete
  operations now use sanitize_object_key instead of sanitize_path_segment
  for the key parameter.

- Cloudflare, DockerHub, GCP, Netlify, PayPal: Migrate validation errors
  to use dedicated InvalidInput error variants with more descriptive
  messages (e.g. "contains path traversal characters" instead of generic
  "contains invalid characters").

- Vercel: Harden client and connector with improved path segment and
  query parameter validation. Tighten error messages across deployments,
  domains, env_vars, and projects sub-modules. Add URL validation for
  webhook endpoints in connector configuration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
